channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 116 replies — 464.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 116 • Updated: 2026-03-24 22:07 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62011878
100$ end of bull run

62014468
You did notice that the activity is actually up by a large % since Acurast launched their corechains (https://data.parity.io/parachain-stats?selection-mode=include&frequency=daily), right guys?

62014587
>>62010191
False rumor about Gavin

62015928
>>62014587
Don't lie. Why do you think they call him WOOD? And he literally looks like a 7yo boy pedo, just look at him. If I googled "Pedophile" then Gavins picture would probably be #2, right after Epstein. And since we're talking about Epstein... Gavin and Epstein were partying all the time together.

62016012
>>62015928
Oh shit. You are a bot

62016043
>>62016012
Could a bot post pictures of his hands with a timestamp? Like I did here; >>62008295 ??? Makes no sense, right?

62016887
>>62016043
So are you for DOT or not

62016901
>>62016887
What do you mean by FOR? Could you define that a little bit more?

62016960
>>61932834
How does a thread with only 94 posts from 20 days ago exist? Dot fag must be bumping it everyday when it hits page 10.

62016999
>>62016960
Read this >>62007000
Also. This is now a DOT board. /biz/ was always a DOT board

62019865
100$ end of bull run

62019872
>>62019865
75

62020329
>>62019872
Dear Punjabista Rajakesh

We talked about this. I first write 100$ and then after it reaches the 9th page you bump the thread and you write 75$. Don't bump the thread before that or Gavin won't pay us DOT for shilling this project.

Best regard from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62021618
>>62016901
What's so hard to understand? Do you support the project?

62023262
>>62021618
>Do you support the project?
In what way? I don't support every decision of the project I guess... But as a whole I really do believe it's the one of the ONLY cryptos worth while. The thing is... Most people don't care about decentralization as a whole and that's why the sector is, in a sense, going under. People got to greedy and lost their roots. Everyone just wants a quick x100 meme coin and that's a big part of the reason there literally was no bull run outside of BTC and a few select meme scam coins. People always destroy everything. It just is how it is. The thing is though. People won't be able to ignore DOT forever. It is what ETH literally wants to be. It has EVERYTHING ETH wants to have in the next 10-15 year. And JAM will probably hit some time this year.

62025332
>>62019872
100$ end of bull run. Now wait until it reaches page 9 and then you bump it Punjabista

62025906
>>62023262
While I agree with the general statement, how can this project hit 100 at the end of the next bull run

62026407
>>62025906
What do you mean "how"? Bro, it was at 55$ when it had literally 0 corechains running. Literally NOTHING worked. And now DOT 1.0 is complete, DOT 2.0 is complete, DOT 3.0 (JAM) is getting shipped this year (I believe). We had a LIVE test of 150k TPS with like 28% of the capability. NOMT + JAM will raise the TPS to MULTIPLE MILLIONS while not raising the fees or lowering the decentralization. There is around 30-50 L2s on the network now, there were 0 corechains when the price was 55$. The inflation dropped significantly and the tokenomics changed favorably, when the price was 55$ the tokenomics were "bad". JAM will introduce continous compute, literally a while(true) loop on-chain. We literally have all the things ETH wants to have but hasn't (shared security, native bridging and L2 interoperability, RISC-V, ...). I could go on and on why this chain is literally the only chain viable for actual real world adoption. We're getting individuality so that bot spam can be prevented. It's the ONLY chain that does what crypto should actually be as a whole. Instead crypto is literally 1000s of projects that are isolated, lonely islands, that can't function together well, that can't safely bridge, that aren't decentralized and if they are they're slow and if they're fast they aren't decentralized. There's always a fucking cut off with every other project whereas with DOT there aren't any cut offs. It just fucking works and it's not an isolated, good-for-nothing, island. Once JAM hits ETH will be able to scale through it. ETH will be able to off-load all the transactions onto DOT and therefore scale into millions of TPS. You do know you don't even need DOT to use corechains? You can use ANY token or DOT. Do you see what I mean? Imagine being able to use DOT on Arbitrum to pay for TX. Well that's the thing... with DOT you can use ARB on Hydration if Hydration implemented it... Like on Bifrost you can use ETH to pay for tx fees. On ETH you can't even do that.

62026423
>>62026407
Yes, but who will be buying? You acknowledged that people just want 200x gains and shitcoins keep oversaturating the market

62026429
>>62025906
Like, the potential of this project is unfathomable. It's lowkey dialed in. it mogs all the other projects. The project is a boon to the whole crypto ecosystem because it does what everyone is trying to do but fails to do. It's stupid how people don't fucking see it. I can just do stuff on here that no other project does. Sure there might be some project that does 1 of the many things DOT can do... But there isn't one project that can do it all outside of DOT. Imagine having L2s on ETH that can NATIVELY bridge and that the bridges were 100% safe. Imagine bridging from Arbitrum to Polygon for less than 1$ and it taking about 30s. Imagine that Arbitrum and Polygon didn't fragment liqudity of ETH. Imagine ARB and Polygon sharing the security of the main network (ETH). Imagine that ARB could use the functionality of Polygon and vice-versa. Imagine that you use 1 singular chain and you can do stuff on other chains while never leaving this singular chain.

>>62026423
>You acknowledged that people just want 200x gains and shitcoins keep oversaturating the market
Yes, and those times are over, aren't they? All the mental illness is flushed out. People can't keep peddling stuff that doesn't do shit and has no value. Look at the DOTcom bubble. Sure there was like a decade long period in the 90s where people scammed everyone and they had the same thing as the meme coin mania in crypto TO BE CONTINUED

62026460
>>62026423
BUT! That shit ended and ONLY the companies that had actual value emerged victorious. Like think about it. There is only so much stupidity people will buy into, once all of that is don with then only real projects will go up. And please tell me this. Do you see any more stupidity after picture NFTs and meme coins? I can't fathom there will be more stupid shit in the coming bull run. Like what are you going to do? meme NFTs? Like actually think about it. There is no more weird, scammy shit left. All that's left is for us to go back to the roots and actually go back to utility. What do you think Amazon will buy into Trump coin or DOGE? No... Amazon and other Web2 companies will buy into REAL products and projects, like DOT. DOT is literally a WEB 3 CLOUD SERVICE PROVIDER. It's the Amazon Cloud killer. Again, once JAM hits we'll have continous compute and therefore a working cloud that's cheaper, faster and INDEPENDENT of Amazon. Imagine Amazon cloud but with NO downtime. Imagine Amazon cloud but you can't get your service taken down if they don't agree with it (so Amazon with no TOS).

You do REALIZE that we're building Web2 from scratch, right? You do REALIZE that that is the ROOT of decentralization. Web3 is just Web2 decentralized. Everything will become decentralized. Decentralized youtube, Decentralized social media, Decentralized cloud providers, ... EVERYTHING WILL BE DECENTRALIZED. Even governments will at some point become decentralized.

What do you think AI agents will buy? BTC and DOT or US treasury bonds? They'll be buying crypto na BILLIONS of AI agents are coming in the coming years. Guys, we're THIS close to getting AGI. What then? Where will AGI get processed? On the MOTHERFUCKING WEB3 CLOUD of course. Web2 clouds are expensive and take time to build. Web3 clouds are cheap and on-demand.

62026470
>>62026460
Valid points, but to convince the crowd, a lot feel like the market is still being manipulated.

BTC and ETH are still the most recognizable crypro out there. What im saying DOT needs to better market itself

62026504
>>62026470
>Valid points, but to convince the crowd
Nope, convince the programmers. They're the ones who decide to be honest. The crowd "doesn't matter". I can give you quick and easy examples. Netflix. Who decided where the Netflix servers are hosted? The programmers or the users? The programmers Netflix uses Amazon Cloud therefore the end-users use Amazon cloud. Are they even AWARE? Most of them aren't, yet they use them. Do you see what I mean? End-users use what the programmers decide is "best". No sane programmer will be like "Yea, let's use ETH. It can handle up to 100 TPS and we need literally tens, hundreds of TPS". Do you see what I mean? That's why I'm saying that DOT is one of the ONLY viable chains IF NOT THE ONLY ONE. Programmers shift stuff. End users just follow what the programmers do. Everything is in the hand of computer engineers nowadays. They run the show now in the new age. AI, robotics, the internet, GPUs, electric cars, crypto, smart cities, ... It's all a computer engineer thing. The end-users are just kettle and followers.

62026694
>>62026504
Im following loud and clear.

Csn the Polka dot team market itself? What good is a platform if it never gets adopted

62026824
>>62026694
What exactly do you mean by "marketing"? They sponsor a F1 car. They also sponsor the team where Messi in in (picrel). They also go around different CS school around the world talking about DOT. They're present on all the major crypto conferences. I mean. You could just google "does polkadot do marketing?" and you'll probably get more stuff. But the thing is. I'm not so sure how effective some of it's marketing really is. It's not like football fans care about decentralization, right? Heineken marketing it's beer is effective, since football fans tend to drink beer, right? Also one of the DOT wallets is being marketed to gamers in CS tournaments if I'm not mistaken...

62027084
>>62026694
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2036502028281663860 They also constantly organize hackatons like these where they hand out prize money for using the protocol itself. So in this case you had to build shit on the newly launched Polkadot hub.
They also have have grants for builders, here: https://polkadot.polkassembly.io/bounty-dashboard.
Also the JAM protocol is going to have about 20 different clients in multiple different languages in order to make the system robust. Most chains literally just have 1 or 2 implementations and if a bug is found in 1 of them then that could be a serious disaster and halt the whole network or even worse... https://jam.web3.foundation/

62027559
>>62027084
I am under the impression that these hackathons garnered little attention and not many participants

62028035
>>62027559
In the tweet (https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2036502028281663860) it literally says
"
More than 260 teams and 850 hackers participated, building with Solidity on Polkadot Hub across EVM and PVM environments.
"
I'd say that's a big turnout for a hackaton with only a 31k$ prize, where the #1 place is literally just 3k$

62028040
>>62027559
pic related

62028045
>>62027559
https://dorahacks.io/hackathon/polkadot-solidity-hackathon/detail

item.title: Nobody wants to buy NYC mansions or townhomes anymore. — 40 replies — 160.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Nobody wants to buy NYC mansions or townhomes anymore.

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-03-24 22:06 UTC

OP

Nobody wants to buy NYC mansions or townhomes anymore.
>at the $15 million and up price point the preference is doorman style apartment buildings, especially if this is a residence and not an investment
Why?

Early replies (first 3)

62013834
Tartaria..

62013851
>>62013830
All the ultra luxury housing in NY is speculative investment. No one lives in those fancy new high rises much less in these "mansions".

62013855
why don't the sellers lower the price

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62014199
>>62014169
rich liberals love the hustle and the bustle
doubt theres any bums loitering around there

62014207
>>62014197
If someone gives you $50M now, you will try to gamble it on shitcoins, you know, to double it. And lose it all to a DEX scam.

62014208
>>62014169
>>62014099
Trying to decide if I want to explain or if I just trust my instincts that these are Israeli glowies.

62014566
>>62013830
> old
> over priced
> falling apart
> require substantial renovations
> cheaper and better options available pretty much anywhere else
> crowded, high property tax area
it really is a mystery isn't it?

62014765
>>62013830
N.

62015476
NYC is a brasil-level shithole to europeans these days. We’d rather livd in the hustle and bustle of our own european cities.

62015477
>>62014099
Yeah exactly, to european standards the metro is fucking ancient and in a very decayed condition.

62015485
>>62014199
Read >>62015476 there are way better cities for hustle and bustle these days. Somehow despite americans living in hyper violent cities with poor infrastructure, their night life is pretty tame and behind in trends compared to europe’s big cities.

62015603
>>62013830
looks like some gangbangers could easily kill you with a driveby shooting and you already put a target on your back buying it

62015736
>>62013874
This, likely. Boomers are in denial that no one can afford their overpriced real estate.

62015742
New york city is really, really crowded. It's hard to move around, either walking, driving, or subway. With that swell comes a lot of noise, dirt, and unpleasant encounters with foreigners.

62018124
>>62014169
Jewyorkers like to visit art galeries, go to theatre, concerts, trendy bars, nightclubs, restaurants...

62018156
>>62018124
Those are literally everywhere.

62022891
Why do chuds laser-focus on the least violent city in the US to talk about crime? NYC is famously much safer than other metros.

62022913
>>62014169
>"New york is the best city in the world"
-someone who's never been to like Tokyo or barcelona or Hong kong or ...

62022968
>>62015742
>really, really crowded
Lol have you been there at all in the past 6 years? There's nobody there except the poors that couldn't afford to leave. Everybody that's still there was just faking it until they made it, which is incredibly shitty advice that stopped being useful 20 years ago. The bridge and tunnel crowd is the only thing keeping it from going under.

62022987
>>62013830
>why aren't richfags buying mansions in a city where the mayor explicitly wants to tax them (and everyone else) to death?!?

62023570
>>62013830
Why is that image so weirdly dark?

62023591
>>62023570
op blocked by the paywall below

62023592
>>62023570
Socio-economic factors.

62024002
>>62023570
Steiny vibes.

62024023
>>62014099
maybe you're foreign or just a hick, but rich people don't use the subway

62024577
>>62013830
Stop posting paywall articles

62024616
>>62024023
Rich people also probably prefer a high rise penthouse or multi floor condo over a street level townhouse in plain view of the street riff raff.

62024673
>>62013830
it's because Mamdani wants to shit diarrhea all over millionaires. Surprise, then millionaires leave for Florida and other based states. whoops. I guess socialism is fucking retarded and doesn't work.

62027174
>>62013830
Weird.
https://reason.com/2026/03/23/new-york-gov-hochul-begs-high-net-worth-refugees-to-return-and-be-taxed/

62027186
These are homes for a very specific niche

>rich people with families

Obviously NYC is attractive to rich people. But as far as families go no, they don't want their kids rubbing shoulders with masses of dirty brown people and feral coons with a socialist brown gypsy mayor.

62027452
>>62015476
What are the best European cities? Like the 2 or 3 best

62028016
>>62024673
East and West Coast are now anti jewish

62028043
>>62013830
It’s weird to live in a 15 million dollar town home on the side of a busy street and sidewalk that crack heads walk by.

item.title: US is bankrupt — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
US is bankrupt

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-03-24 22:03 UTC

OP

US is bankrupt

Early replies (first 3)

62026401
>borrow 40 trillion dollars
>spend it on building the most powerful military in history
>the debt collectors ask for the money back
>use your military to kill them

62026472
>>62026388
I voted for this

62026477
>>62026388
Powell should have lowered rates

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62026489
>>62026388
>steve hanke
>Sep 23 2022
>According to CNBC’s September Fed survey of economists, fund managers and strategists, those surveyed said there’s a 52% chance that U.S. could enter into recession over the next 12 months.

>“The probability of recession, I think it’s much higher than 50% — I think it’s about 80%. Maybe even higher than 80%,” Hanke told CNBC’s “Street Signs Asia” on Friday.

lol that was literally the best time to buy

62026512
>>62026401
>chud discovers the "debt schizos are morons" talkingpoint

62026513
>>62026388
... and finished

62026660
>>62026489
2022 recession fud was wild.

62026692
>>62026401
Don’t bother looking into who the US government owes money to. DEFINITELY don’t bother looking into intra-governmental debt either.

62026696
>>62026388
>Good times!
Nothing screams a good times like zero job growth, double digit stealth inflation, shrinkflation and reduced quality of food, alcohol and goods.

62026813
>>62026388
Not really. The US has plenty of oil and a net exporter at that. The price of oil is steadily increasing meaning the US is profiting bigly from the ME conflict. Why bankrupt when the economy is pretty much alive? Europe on the other hand, is not gonna make it with how little oil reserves they have left.

62026850
>>62026813
That has nothing to do with beeing bankrupt. Oil is not everything.

62026869
>>62026388
>Liberal experts say "TRUMP IS DESTROYING EVERYTHING!"
OMG I GUESS I BETTER PANIC SELL EVERYTHING!!!!

62026880
>>62026660
Jaime Dimon same thing lmao
These economists are retards just guessing

62026914
>>62026477
the high rates are the sticky inflation

62026917
>>62026401
>spend it on building the most powerful military in history
Still waiting for that military

62027191
>>62026401
>Default on debt payments
>70% of bond holder are your own citizens and national companies

Ok retard

62027216
>>62026917
Fr. It's all mutts who wanted free education, and low iq white goyslaves who were born into "proud" veteran slave households. Our military is shit.

62027398
>>62026880
I remembered pointing out the tribe memebership of these tow authors to doomers back then. But they were too retarded tho antisemitic but the phrase that always comes is
>this time they are right

62027478
>>62026692
>>62027191
>he thinks the government cares about its citizens

62027523
>>62027222
Checked

62027536
>>62026401
Our military is a shadow of what it once was. It's certainly not WWII level, it's not even Bush 2 level anymore. Obama/Trump/Biden era killed what was left of the US military. A miltiary that exists solely to move money to favored donors and interests will inevitably atrophy and wither because all the blood is going to parasites. All that's left is a half-baked invasion of a foreign country before it all falls to ruin and people start snubbing us and people stop signing up without massive inflationary payments to entice people to enlist into a crumbling lockmart welfare program.

62027541
>>62026388
What does that even mean? So what they're never gonna stop spending until they have to

62027666
The world economy has been running on fumes since 2008
then they did the covid scam to steal money and try to turn society into chaos. Then they literally printed trillions overnight in 2020.
Everything after that is a slow descent to hell, and there's no escape outside of total destruction of the USA and europe

62028022
>>62026869
anon... he's the one who got into power using the debt as an argument lol

item.title: WHAT THE FUCK WAS THAT OIL BROS??? — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHAT THE FUCK WAS THAT OIL BROS???

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-24 22:15 UTC

OP

WHAT THE FUCK WAS THAT OIL BROS???

Early replies (first 3)

62027707
Some jewish Israeli news agency said that Kushner and Witkoff (who Iran won't negotiate with anyway) are planning a '15 step deal'. Just more jawboning nonsense to keep the price down. Wait 20 mins and the Iranians will say it's horseshite

62027753
Stop betting on the price of oil to go up. If oil goes up enough for you to make money that'd be a disaster for everyone. But even if the price of oil stays the same or even if it goes down a bit oil companies will make more money. That could translate to more buybacks and dividends

62027814
>>62027614
its called the thanks for playing goyim

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62027838
>>62027614
>Let the goyim deal in the real world

62027892
>>62027707
>15 step deal
Because it totally worked in Russia Ukraine war.

62027930
>>62027753
It's because most here don't have a clue about buybacks and dividends and just speculate on whatever meme asset is going up. I've been saying something similar for ages, that it's wiser to invest in oil companies than the futures.

62027939
>>62027753
>If oil goes up enough for you to make money that'd be a disaster for everyone.
Good.

62027964
>>62027892
How much you wanna bet they just cooked it up in chat goy pee tea

62027971
>>62027753
The psychical barrels are going up in price. It's just the benchmarks that are being suppressed in an attempt to control markets. There's literally shortages and rationing going on across Asia right now but somehow oil is below $100

62028057
>>62027838
how do we recover from this quote. Its just so brutal

62028077
>>62027939
lumi no put it down

item.title: What are the chances of a postwar pump? — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What are the chances of a postwar pump?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-24 22:08 UTC

OP

Assuming the war wraps up within the next month with either a total Iranian victory and Trump chickening out and returning to status quo ante, or a marginal US victory with Iran being forced back into the cuckshed with shipping returning to normal, what are the chances that we see a recovery on the back of massive inflation, as happened during the Covid lockdown?

Early replies (first 3)

62024719
None, the covid recovery was made through mass money printing. They won't turn the printer on to that scale unless things are very bad.

62024739
Inflation already going to happen , energy prices contribute to the price over everything and energy will not get cheaper despite bussyant trying to artificially lower the futures price of oil.

62024740
Liquidity is dried up because of treasury shorting. No more money as it burned up unless if you’re an insider.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62024748
day 25 of 3 day war
>assume the war wraps up within the month

62024782
>>62024703
The effects of this war will be intergenerational, covid will be remembered as a small slip in the economy.

62025961
>>62024719
I wouldn't put it past this admin

62026171
>>62024719
Crazy recovery bounces happened many times after covid

62026176
>>62024719
>the covid recovery was made through mass money printing
guess what's happening next

62027952
>>62024703
Very likely. There are juicy IPOs coming this year starting in June i believe.
So another month or so of scary shit and only when you are 100% convinced that the whole world is going to shit you buy.

62028050
>>62024703
It's stagflation time baby, no quick rebound from that.

item.title: HE SOLD COVERED CALLS ON THE BITCOIN THEY NUKED THE PRICE AND HE SAID COOL AND COLLECTED THE PREMIUM WHILE THEY SIT OTM… — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HE SOLD COVERED CALLS ON THE BITCOIN THEY NUKED THE PRICE AND HE SAID COOL AND COLLECTED THE PREMIUM WHILE THEY SIT OTM…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-24 22:14 UTC

OP

HE SOLD COVERED CALLS ON THE BITCOIN
THEY NUKED THE PRICE AND HE SAID COOL AND COLLECTED THE PREMIUM WHILE THEY SIT OTM
GET ABSOLUTELY FUUUUUUUUUCKED LMAO

Early replies (first 3)

62027636
What the fuck does any of that even mean?

62027663
>>62027613
Imagine being in 2026 and still not knowing how to speak or write in English?

62027672
explain this to me as if i was an xrp holder

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62027678
>>62027613
calm down
breathe

>>62027636
he's talking about gamestop

62027691
>>62027636
He is trying to be relevant.

62027720
>basic bitch options trading
Nice to see. Wish I saw more of this.

62027734
>>62027672
May I speak with your wife's boyfriend?

62028076
>>62027678
I BEEN BREATHING SON HOW DO YOU THINK I'M SHOUTING SO LOUD MOTHERFUCKER

item.title: IT'S BURSTING — 11 replies — 40.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT'S BURSTING

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-24 22:00 UTC

OP

SELL SELL SELL SELL SELL

Early replies (first 3)

62027829
>rest of economy is allowed to sell drugs, porn, gambling
>Country ruled by pedophiles
>OH MY GOD WE MUST PROTECT THE PURITAN SPIRIT OF THE UNITED STATES BAN AI GENERATED PORN IMMEDIATELY
Doesn't make sense to me personally.

62027839
NOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO IT WAS KINO

62027852
It is a money sink though. The kind of thing only Elon Musk could bankroll

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62027855
>>62027829
It's closing because it's a huge money pit and they are shifting their focus to compete with Anthropic in coding, they even announced they also announced a shift away from owning data centres to merely renting them

62027856
>>62027821
Remember when google shut down that website which streams games?

62027862
>>62027855
no, it's the jews

62027863
>>62027829
>>62027855
They've been slashing costs left and right because they're doing an IPO later this year, the data centre build out is a mirage, there's simply not enough capital, energy and material to do it

https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/22/openai-data-center-pivot-underscores-wall-street-ipo-concerns.html

62027871
>>62027862
Sam Altman is a Jew though

62027898
>>62027863
they have infinite money. they just realized it was too good prodact to give us . they don't want goyim have access to good tecnology

62027904
>>62027898
OpenAI doesn't have infinite money LOL, not even the likes of Google or META have infinite money and have issued bonds worth billions of dollars to fund this spend

62028008
SORA more like SAYONARA

item.title: SMCI calls April 2 expiration 30c trading at 0.04 Thank me later — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SMCI calls April 2 expiration 30c trading at 0.04 Thank me later

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-24 22:12 UTC

OP

all yall niggas need to sell covered calls on smci. naked calls even.

62027920

item.title: it's over — 49 replies — 28.6/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-03-24 20:33 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62020090

62020123
>$64 trillion baked in
Fucking hell

62020245
eh I wouldn't worry about it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62024118
>>62020044
what's the point of saying
>for the first time in history
?
it only goes up so the latest number will always be the first in history

62024312
>>62020044
Please forgive my financial ignorance, what's the breaking point? Where does everything go sideways?

62024879
>>62020361
The US government and ADL are doing overtime trying to shut down the "won't die for Israel" narrative.

62024927
>>62024879
why are arabs and antisemites literally so obsessed with israel? is it like some sort of inferiority complex? why do you simp for sandnigger muzzies against innocent white women?

62024996
>>62024312
when the quality of life for the peasants drops so much that they chimp out

62025047
>>62024312
Just like the fall of the Roman Empire, there isn't really one point it stopped existing, at least nominally. The USD will just keep losing value until it stops being attractive to migrate to the US since their absolute salaries will be low compared to the rest of the world. Eventually they will lose their leading edge on knowledge and technology. At one point, we will see a brain drain OUT of the US for the first time. They will be so poor that they won't be able to afford importing stuff they need for their military. We can already see how dependant they are on imports for computer chips. They will turn into some sort of a big Brazil and will finally split into two to separate countries again, this time permanently. The liberal one will eventually collapse and balkanize, while the conservative one will hang on and slowly die out centuries later.
I doubt these will happen before 2100, and for that reason I still invest in the US stock market. There's a lot of money to siphon out of that place just yet.

62025058
>>62020090
There are 10 trillion in unfunded pension liabilities because of BOOMERS LOL coming due every year.

It's either oyu kill your parents or they jack taxes u to 50% (not on elon) to pay for your parents to keep partying until they have dementia and need a 24/7 nurse.

62025067
>>62024927

62025155
>>62024927
It's almost like aipac bribes all our politicians and makes them go kiss a wall and sell the usa out to kikes or something...

The epstein hasnt got much longer left. People know what kikes think of goyims now.

62025425
>>62025155
bribes are illegal tho

62025431
>>62025425
lol, lmao

62025462
>>62021448
>groypers infiltrating institutions
>white military forming separate militias
>right as the republic reached the oligarchy stage
the history books that cover the rest of our lives will not be boring, at least

62025479
>>62025431
literally you will bitch and moan about muh aipac bribing politicians
1) bribes are illegal, so just call the cops on them if thats what they are doing
2) elect politicians who wont take bribes
israel is just that badass that all americans love it, like its an oasis surrounded by these dark ppl

62025491
>>62025479
Bribes aren't illegal dumbass, what do you think lobbying is. That's why US has very low levels of political corruption, because corruption was made legal, it's just legal to take bribes in US

62025522
>>62025491
retard. lobbying isnt bribing. just admit you hate israel lol

62025539
>>62025058
Same parents who had me circumcised
Same parents who either/or
>reeeeee about ice and the poor little browns
>xtians who support Israel and zognald

There might not even be a civilization when it's all said and done. Civilization and all modern fiat will die with the boomers.

62025587
>>62020044
this has been said for 50 years dude. nothing will happen.
>2030
>omg us debt is 549 trillion it's gonna collapse any second now!!!

62025623
OH MY GOD, THE US DEBT IS INCREASING, THIS IS UNHEARD OF

62025633
>>62020361
>her

62025647
>entire economy is based on debt
>90% of debt is owed to the US
if you are worried about the debt you are fucking retarded

62025861
>>62020555
Not a comforting comparison for several well-known reasons.

90% of Japanese government debt is held domestically — by Japanese banks, insurance companies, pension funds, and the Bank of Japan itself. This means Japan is essentially borrowing from itself, so there's no foreign creditor pressure, no currency flight risk, and little chance of a sudden foreign selloff. US debt by contrast has significant foreign ownership (China, Japan, and others hold large portions), making it more vulnerable to geopolitical pressure.

The BOJ owns an extraordinary share of Japanese government bonds through decades of quantitative easing. A government effectively owing money to its own central bank is categorically different from owing it to external creditors.

Japan is the world's largest net creditor — it owns more foreign assets than foreigners own Japanese assets. This means the gross debt figure is offset by enormous foreign holdings.
The US by contrast is the world's largest net debtor nation.

Also, Japanese government debt is held at very low interest rates. But the US is moving at an accelerating pace towards the point where the interest on the debt alone exceeds all revenue.

And Japanese households have traditionally maintained very high savings rates, providing a large domestic pool of capital to absorb government debt. In the USA, on the other hand, the people are up to their eyeballs in their own personal debt and are defaulting at an accelerating pace.

So the USA is actually far more fucked by its debt than Japan.

62027225
>>62025539
Great innit?

My mom was somehow simultaneously a feminist AND a religious christian in highschool so she had double the reasons to cockblock me in every way possible. Missed out on teen pussy because of that psycho.
>BUT WHY ARE YOU SO MENA TO ME NOW SON
SHUT THE FUCK UP BITCH, DON'T CALL ME WHEN I'M DRIVING HOME FROM WORK AT 10 PM BECAUSE I HAVE 3 JOBS

62027228
>>62025647
>>62025861
The US won't collapse, it will jack up taxes on (You) and everyone younger to get back their money, because hte debt was taken out against the future generations.

62027274
>>62020044
this is good

62027392
>>62020044
A debt crisis doesn't necessarily mean the economy goes kaput. They can just inflate the debt away if need be. If the US Gov buys assets like Gold for $5000 and then decides that they are going back to a gold standard where Gold = $10,000 suddenly they have doubled their money, but really they just made the currency worth half as much. This helps with debt though because the debts are still at face value so they can just sell the gold back at the new prices and pay the debts.

Now imagine instead of making it that obvious they buy a bunch of gold at $5000 and then over the course of some years they allow it to rise to $50k. That is 10x and doesn't technically seem like a devaluation occurred, but the assets owned by the government suddenly exceed their liabilities so the government is solvent again.

Even if they don't plan on doing this if the government needs to pay for something really bad they can just print so this might end up happening without any plan for it to happen. Even technically speaking all the scrap steel in the navy might eventually be an asset worth more than all the debt used to pay for it if a commodities go up in price sufficiently.

The problem is that a commodity boom is terrible for the rest of the economy because everyone who needs anything needs to pay the high commodity prices

62027459
>>62024927
g8 b8 m8
the brainwashing no longer works lmao
death to israel

62027463
let's just forgive all debts and move on

62027480
>>62020044
Why did the Supreme Court do this?

62027500
>>62020044
See you bros at the rations line

62027547
>>62025047
US salaries are good though? I mean the whole reason people get paid more here versus some place like Japan is primarily due to inflation.

>online dating is the future of lo-ACK!

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-03-24 17:52 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62017513
>>62017496
We already had this thread. Bumble is merely a lesson in why women are retarded.

62017539
Its was just a way to spread stds to a control group of retards

62017623
>>62017496
Are these dating apps even useable on the free tiers? Any time I install one of these you literally cannot do anything unless you pay them some absurd monthly fee like $29 a month. Who actually pays for this crap?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62021380
>>62018101
>>62018864
>>62019087
You all are just jealous

62021435
>>62017496
They got greedy, and it's all full of scammers.

62021478
>>62017623
I used Bumble free and I married a girl I met on there.
2 kids as well.
These apps just match you to actual people. I don't understand the seethe, unless you're ugly of course.

62021549
>>62017496
>Dating app crashes when women realize they can just onlyfags instead
Woah

62021571
>>62021478
You must have been using it in the 2010s. It’s all bots and microtransactions now

62022431
>>62017702
I want a virgin wife, though, not some degenerate whore. casual sex is unpleasant desu.

62023902
>>62019161
Oof a random fag makes u list ur life achievments. I got second hand embarassment from reading ur shit brah.

62023951
>>62017496
Time to pick up some cheapies!

62023997
>>62021049
An 18-year-old wearing that big-ass cubic zirconia on her pointer finger?
I don't think so.

62024069
>>62017702
kys shill

62024197
>>62017623
I think I spent about $600 on Tinder almost a decade ago.

It resulted in hookups with 15 girls.
Many of which I hooked up with multiple times.
Some of them were longer FWB situations.
A couple turned into actual relationships.

Even if you assume minimal ROI (only hooking up once) it cost me $40 per girl.
AND the premium features also saved me a lot of time on the app, that's ROI too.
Most of the money was spent in short bursts while abroad on trips when time was limited and I wanted to maximize my pull. I'm pretty sure I'd have missed these opportunities if I wasn't premium boosted to the max and all that.

$600 sounds like a lot of money but $40 a girl really is an amazing deal in my book.
The time sink the app still was is where the pain is, the grind that is dealing with all the flaking and ghosting. THAT is what sucks.

If you'd give me a machine that took $40 and delivered me a fun girl who is looking to hookup every single time I'd pay without hesitation until the end of time.

inb4 poorfags complain $40/g is a lot of money. $600 isn't even, literal peanuts for living out some great sexual fantasies and farming memories for a lifetime.

62024432
>>62019188
These dating sites were plagued with adulterers since the 90's. It was only ever good for people in their late teens to meet each other after 2000. That is where your nostalgia and lack of attention to what was going on with older people trying to find love during those brief glory days. I never tried it to avoid dating someone who was married.

62024446
>>62024197
>spent about $600 on Tinder almost a decade ago.
I agree wholeheartedly 100% with the pay approach and have done it myself, but the issue is that $600 won't get you nearly as far as it did 10 years ago due to the failing company greed and algo changes.

62024454
>>62017710
>indians and other non-whites
Serious question; how are white women supposed to use these sites if they always have 800 likes from India everyday?

62024523
dating apps became shitty because they got flooded with normies

a decade ago online dating was still somewhat taboo, you could pick up some hot alt chick looking a for a slutty thrill with minimal effort cuz online dating gave normies the ick

now every one and their mother is on the app, competition is so high you have better odds RETVRNing to tradition and trying your luck at a bar or night club

62024730
Why would I do any of that shit when cocaine is cheap and porn is free? I can stim goon all night and not have to deal with any of the bullshit of dealing with a woman.

62025482
>>62021571
Met her in 2022.

62025787
>>62017496
For those that want good results on dating apps here is some data from 2017 hinge to help increase match rate.

1. Black and white photo
2. Action video
3. Short to the point bio 1-2 sentences
4. Hide education if bachelors or lower
5. Veterinarian, MD, and pilot careers get most matches
6. 6foot + in height
7. Pet pics (dog beats cat) but a horse will beat out all pets and all previous listed information
8. A boat pic will single handedly beat horse height and career and all other listed information. A rowboat will get you matches more that a sports car.

PS this probably won't work now due to today's politics but setting your ethnicity or religion to Jewish will increase your match rate.

62025932
>>62017702
Unless you live in populated dense areas tinder and other dating apps suck

62025946
Is paying for dating apps a rip off for most people?

Chads need not respond.

62026034
What are the odds of finding someone like her GOD DAMN she build for breeding Kek got more insane tinder bumble pulls lol

62026279
>>62026034
you can find women like her at the gym, anon.

62026546
>>62017702
i don't want a hookup, hookups suck, i want a long term lover...

>>62021193
this age can be lowered to 25 if you're in a rural area. But yeah I'm 29 and the FOMO is real. Fuck fuck fuck. What do I do, what do I do, what do I do. Bros.. I don't want to have to choose between fat girls and single moms with negro babies. Apps aren't working and most mutual acquaintances "hey I have this single friend" have fallen through.

62026616

62026623

62026639

https://youtu.be/SbQu55bMvZs?t=1566

STOP APPROACHING WOMEN. STOP USING ONLINE APPS. STOP GIVING WOMEN ATTENTION

Stop Asking Women On Dates, stop texting them thinking you are interesting because you are not. The reason you are getting ghosted is because you text and try to be funny, try to make her laugh or sound interesting. If you do manage to go to the next step and are actually meeting up and then get ghosted, stop taking women on dates. That stuff doesn't work anymore in the current year.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pyEdsrfTd6Q

Most importantly stop approaching women, at least for a while. They are already seething and this will only get worse and worse as they get less and less attention from men.
If men can't display a modicum of power over society, this world and women, then I guess they don't hold much power at all.

item.title: Dont do meth + trade boys — 39 replies — 11.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Dont do meth + trade boys

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-03-24 18:59 UTC

OP

Last year october, while I was eating meth all day everyday i decided to put 500k (my entire lfiesavings) all on ETH.. I am now down to about 200k and its not looking great.

Learn from me gentlemen. I am now genuinely considering those switzerland assisted euthanasia pods if things dont go up soon

Early replies (first 3)

62024576
>>62024567
yikes

62024595
Just don't become an addict. Alcohol, drugs... I was an alcoholic for 10 years and it ruined my life.

Funny thing, I became an alcoholic for being a wagie. I got the night shift because the salary is good and became unable to sleep. I just couldn't sleep. Started drinking to sleep. Then one day I threatened my boss (alcohol rage). Now, I'm not a wagie and I'm not an alcoholic. I'm nothing.

62024602
>>62024567
>eating meth all day everyday
the fuck? I do the same, 90% racemic p2p based methamphetamine hydrochloride

5-25mg a day. im dying.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62024666
>>62024635

Goddamn nigga thats some silent hill shit

62024669

62024929

satan digits

seriously though you know what im talking about. there was NO WAY IN FUCKING HELL im gonna look at the dark corner in my room if im aware im hallucinating, I couldn't help but notice strange squirming centipede like creatures on my desk that would go away if I looked close enough. there was no fucking way I was gonna investigate. When I woke up everything was fine.

>>62024659

interesting. for me it was like... a literal movie like filter, like webs just popping in and out and scattered like art, i knew it was a hallucination because it made no medical sense.

62024931
>>62024666

by the way I did this. no heart palpitations and my hands arent blue yet. might do another 7mg IDK though, lmk

62024945
>>62024567

62024956
>>62024945
honestly bro I get fucking annoyed when I get horny on meth, you get ultra horny just eating it too; I can't imagine the people who fucking smoke it or whatever other ROA. Like wanting to just fuck NOW or jack off NOW, you have to either get into work fast or somehow avoid seeing/hearing women. When you're trying to get work done, it's really annoying.

The most jarring thing about eating meth, there is no comedown. It's very surreal. I get why they say it's "clean", but it's an illusion and you need to take care of yourself before you slip into insanity (mania).

disclaimer: look you can do whatever with your life, but please... if anything never ever ever ever inject meth; you will kill your kidneys, and if you dont you will enlarge and permanently damage your heart. Don't do it ever please. That being said I only read about it, I never did it myself. I only eat it (Oral ROA).

62025233
>>62024956
glad to see im not the only meth-eater in town

62025245
>>62025233

most bitter taste in EXISTENCE dude, WHY is it so bitter GOD damn.

62025246
>>62025233
cant stop fcking SCRATCHING MY HEAD GOD DAMNIT!!! my head... im gonna literally scratch all the hair off my head I NEED TO STOP GOD MY HEAD HURTS I cant stop scratching my headddddd

62025249

62025258
>>62025245

62025268
>>62025258
I be fuckin (not sexually rn atm) getting my milligram weighing scale and just fucking SPLIT THEM CRYSTALINOS until I get the right amount, it works for me! then I just sweep the dusty shit into a bowl and it never really gets wasted. Ah just meth dust all over my table. Caffeine powder too (guarana seed baby).

I use blate papes if I need to but just putting them on the tongue is enough. urgh i was gonna show you a pic of it IRL to show you im not shitting you but I cant find it rn sorry my /biz/igga (/biz/ nigga)

62025279
>>62024669

I'm thinking about getting back in just as a hedge against losing my job to AI.

62025284
>>62025268
i can tell ur on sum electrifying shit

62025294
>>62025284
the limit to oral meth: FORGET the mg amount, 10,20,30,40,50,60 FUCKING forget it (actually dont, just cap out at 40mg at once; that's a lot, dont die)

the real limit:
>are your hands blue? STOP.
>heart arrhythmias? STOP.
>traumas resurfacing? STOP.
>eyes popping out? STOP.
etc

once you master that... well it's still fucking bad but ur not really dying. only 3% of meth users are oral. not a lot of data but the slow rise in dopamine implies far lower neurotoxicity. however the vasoconstriction is EXTREMELY bad.

oh goddddd oh godddddd ooohh hohh ogdddddddddddddddd

62025295
>>62025294
>>are your hands blue? STOP.
i remember once i binged and my fingers were blue

that only happened that one time, but man that fucking alarmed me

62025298
>>62025295
>but man that fucking alarmed me
happened to me at a party last friday, I get you. I just remember feeling very guilty, I was born so healthy. we were all born healthy I presume, and it's like we're violating our bodies like this. It's not right. I remember hiding my hands so that nobody noticed, but I looked down I was like "fuck, too much.", indeed it was; like 60mg in a 48 hour span. It really is not worth it.

at least it aint mdpv

62025379
I CANT FUCKING REMEMBER IF I TOOK 10MG MORE OR MORE OR NOT

62025399

62025407

62026933

IM STILL AWAKKE AHHAHYAHAHAHAHA

62026946

62027041
>>62026933

I wanna tell you dont fuckking feel guilty bro you are fine, listen listen to this if you wanna feel better because this is how I feel right now. I feel great: https://youtu.be/6-8E4Nirh9s

I'm gonna crash maybe 6-8 hours, I have to because there's a part of my mind that know it's not okay to be awake for 2.5 days straight. I think I slept on sunday right?

62027049
>>62027041

62027058
>>62026933

you soundcool and I remember everything I just, im not myself

item.title: US is insolvent? — 9 replies — 10.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
US is insolvent?

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-03-24 21:24 UTC

OP

US is insolvent?

Early replies (first 3)

62024380
nah the whole thing is a nothingburger, eventually Iran will just stop on their own.

62025021
>>62024364
Idk idc

62025063
OH N-

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62027277
>>62024364
They can print as much money as they want.
Or not?

62027311
>>62024364
literally not possible as >>62027277
has pointed out

the dollar will become worthless sure, but it's not possible for the US to default on anything

62027475
>>62027311
You are correct that the US government will always be able to meet its existing contractual obligations denominated in US dollars. But it can still be insolvent in the sense of being unable to continue to conduct its business for lack of funds. If the dollar goes to zero, the government will literally not be able to keep the lights on because it will have no funds that the power company will accept. As soon as a contract denominated in dollars terminates it will not be renewed. The government will not be able to purchase weapons or pay soldiers. It will not be able to hire any new employees as old ones quit in disgust. Although technically not bankrupt, it will be insolvent in the sense of lacking funds with which to conduct business.

62027709
I wonder what the new fed chair has to say about this
maybe he will quantify some easing into the markets?

62027735
>>62027277
>>62027311
>>62027475
Not at all, retards lol
Do you mutts live under a rock? Russia had to default not because they lacked US dollars, but because they had been banned from every payment system and literally couldn't pay their debt. If muttistan decides to just start printing dollaridoos, you'll be banned from making payments as well lol

62027834
>>62027735
damn you are stupid, I can tell you're Russian so it makes sense, see the difference here is that the US is the one that owns and runs all those payment systems, the federal reserve literally controls all of that, all debts are in dollars and can be contractually paid with dollars.

item.title: ETERNAL REMINDER: PRECIOUS METALS ARE NOT INVESTMENT — 5 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
ETERNAL REMINDER: PRECIOUS METALS ARE NOT INVESTMENT

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-03-24 21:44 UTC

OP

Consider this a PSA for all the spiritual dwarves in /biz/ scamming people into buying rock collections.

For all you have insulted my crypto, your rock collection is rugging harder than the worst of them. PMs is such a bad investment, it loses to Pokémon cards for any time period.

Eternal reminder:
>the bottom of Bitcoin is 65k
>the bottom of Gold is 10 dollars
>Fed ends the silver
>ETFs and treasury bonds outperform PMs without a fraction of the stress and mental illness
>Gold cannot be used to buy groceries, therefore it is useless
>your PMs are not even real, they are a company paper promissing you that the metal exists

PMs provide nothing, besides an accurate metric of how much market leverage is in the hands of the mentally unwell.

OP

>Roughly 15 minutes later, at 7:05 a.m., Trump posted a market-moving announcement about Iran on Truth Social.

jannies do your goddamn job

62026444

62026839
>>62026406
well, kikes manipulating markets...
Look at this miga shill.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62026854
>>62026390
>Everything is insiders and le hekkin manipooooooooliation!
Yaaaawn. Get some new material.

62026904
>>62026854
post nose, jew
the only ones not absolutely flipping shit over this are jews that are insider trading.
when everyone finds out how manipulated this all is and where their retirments went, jews are going to burn

62027021
cope posts >>62026406 >>62026854 lmao
go deal in the real world, faggots

62027030
Dude he literally flat out rugpulled his shitcoin in broad daylight. Who would be surprised by this shit? This is the same admin that bombed a country twice during “negotiations” career politicians get my vote for the rest of my life, I thought there was a bottom to moral decay and decency

62027039
>>62027030
where should i put my money if the SP500 & Crypto is rigged?

62027074
>>62026390
Isn't preventing this kind of thing the reason that the SEC exists?

62027708
>>62027074
Sec is a meme, its just another bunch of babayeater jews.

item.title: Who’s buying BTC at the dip? — 10 replies — 6.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who’s buying BTC at the dip?

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-24 20:41 UTC

OP

Dip incoming, time to stack or wait it out?
Share your moves, bags, or strategies. Let’s see who’s taking advantage of the dip.

Early replies (first 3)

62025264
>>62025259
Might wait another couple of months...

62025266
>>62025264
Try a year.

62025274
>>62025264
Like how many months?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62025292
>>62025259
I'm buying aggressively. Everyone and their mom is waiting to buy at an October bottom, so it's going to be a few months earlier than that. I'll be slurping it till the end of summer probably.

62025378
>>62025266
Let's see if you're right

62025418
>>62025259
Just wait for the next dump. This weekend or next Trump will threaten Iran on Friday night or Saturday and TACO before market open on Monday.

62026385
>>62025292
October bottom?
How sure??

62026568
>>62025259
Just started buying a small amount weekly

62027601
>>62025418
but Iran isn't afraid tho. Gotta prepare for what's comin

62027610
BTCISTERS WHY CAN'T WE BREAK THROUGH 70K???
